note: LLM in the loop: Claude (claude-opus-4-8) ranked the vetted rescue paths and the executor sent its choice to a live EVM. The deterministic core sized every path and enforced the spend cap + action whitelist BEFORE ranking, so the model's only freedom was which safe path to take.
scenario: mEURC collateral, USDC debt; EURC drifts down until HF crosses the Mandate trigger

viablePaths.why: TOP-UP 3.00 USDC from reserve (no swap, no slippage, no collateral sold) — PARTIAL: only reaches HF 1.266 (target 1.380, capped)
DELEVERAGE: sell 13.97 USDC of mEURC, repay debt

ranking.memo: HF 1.190 < trigger 1.200. Target 1.380. Debt 50.00 USDC. Candidates: TOP-UP, ROTATE, DELEVERAGE. LLM ranked 3 viable paths and chose ROTATE 47.50 USDC of mEURC(LT 0.700) → mTBILL(LT 0.900) (cost 0.36 USDC). Reason: ROTATE fully restores health to the 1.380 target for just 0.36 USDC by swapping into higher-quality collateral, keeping your market exposure intact. The TOPUP only reaches 1.266 (still at risk), and DELEVERAGE costs 17.69 USDC and permanently shrinks your position — neither is warranted when a cheap durable fix exists.
